nodes.label: CALCULATING EMPIRES
nodes.summary: The central project: a visual genealogy of technology and power since 1500.

content.title: Calculating Empires
content.subtitle: A genealogy of technology and power since 1500
paragraphs: Calculating Empires is a large-scale research visualization that places contemporary technical systems within longer histories of power.
This prototype treats the project as the central node through which documented research, structural analysis, and personal interpretation can be navigated.

nodes.label: AUTHORS AND GENEALOGY
nodes.summary: The separate trajectories of Kate Crawford and Vladan Joler before their convergence.

content.title: Prior trajectories
content.subtitle: Research, institutions, visual practice, and collaboration
paragraphs: This section maps the distinct backgrounds and earlier projects that precede Calculating Empires.
Documented facts are separated from inferred continuities and unresolved research gaps.

nodes.label: METHODOLOGY
nodes.summary: How historical material becomes an interactive genealogy.

content.title: How the project is constructed
content.subtitle: From historical inquiry to interactive genealogy
paragraphs: The workflow moves through research, organization, visual encoding, and interaction.
Unpublished archival, bibliographic, image-selection, and cross-referencing procedures remain explicitly marked as research gaps.

nodes.label: PROJECT STRUCTURE
nodes.summary: Four themes, temporal and systemic axes, and a two-map organization.

content.title: The organizing system
content.subtitle: Communication, computation, classification, and control
paragraphs: This section will describe the documented taxonomy, temporal axis, systems axis, diptych, and navigation model.
Detailed structural analysis will be expanded in the next research stage.

nodes.label: RHETORICAL ANALYSIS
nodes.summary: How scale, density, juxtaposition, and navigation produce an argument.

content.title: Argument through form
content.subtitle: How the visualization persuades
paragraphs: This analytical layer will examine how visual density, historical juxtaposition, diagrammatic authority, and flexible reading shape the project's claims.
Its relations are interpretive unless directly supported by project statements.

nodes.label: WIDER PRACTICE
nodes.summary: The project within wider research, exhibition, and critical-visualization practices.

content.title: A wider field
content.subtitle: Research visualization, installation, and critical cartography
paragraphs: This section will place the work in relation to critical visualization, exhibition practice, and public research formats.
Only documented institutional and exhibition relationships will be treated as factual.

nodes.label: PERSONAL ASSESSMENT
nodes.summary: The author's explicit personal evaluation of the precedent.

content.title: Critical evaluation
content.subtitle: A clearly separated personal position
paragraphs: This section is reserved for the student's own critical assessment.
It remains visually and evidentially distinct from documented project information.

nodes.label: GRID AS APPARATUS
nodes.summary: A personal theoretical lens for reading the grid as an apparatus of ordering.

content.title: The grid is not neutral
content.subtitle: A personal interpretive proposition
paragraphs: This section will test the grid as an apparatus that orders knowledge, visibility, comparison, and power.
The proposition belongs to the author's critical interpretation, not to the documented intentions of Crawford and Joler.

nodes.label: SOURCES
nodes.summary: Official descriptions, biographies, credits, publications, and research gaps.

content.title: Evidence base
content.subtitle: Documentation, citations, and verification status
paragraphs: The source layer supports factual nodes and records where evidence is incomplete.
It prevents inferred relationships from appearing as documented facts.

nodes.label: POSTER
nodes.summary: A later visual output translating the critical reading into a poster.

content.title: Visual synthesis
content.subtitle: A design output developed from the precedent study
paragraphs: The poster will visualize the personal interpretation rather than reproduce the original project.
Its content and form remain placeholders at this prototype stage.
